archimate:Grouping.name: Schedule Management Enablers
archimate:Grouping.documentation:
Definition: Schedule Management Enablers ABB is a Grouping that enables the implementation of components and services to create, distribute and manage events at different scales inside a cloud platform.
Source: Inspired in Redhat
Source reference: https://www.redhat.com/en/topics/cloud-computing/what-is-cloud-management
Example: The following implementation is an example on how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): Chronos is a replacement for cron. It is a distributed and fault-tolerant scheduler that runs on top of Apache Mesos that can be used for job orchestration. It supports custom Mesos executors as well as the default command executor. Thus by default, Chronos executes sh (on most systems bash) scripts. https://sourceforge.net/projects/chronos.mirror/
Interoperability Dimension: Structural IoP
LOST view: Technical view - application
Interoperability Saliency: The Schedule Management Enablers Grouping ABB is salient for technical interoperability because it comprises the different services and tools that allow to organise and manage events within a platform for the delivery of a digital public service. It enables interoperability by allowing the management of events through a single point of access (for users and administrations) and the reuse of such information to an overall relationship between public administration and stakeholders.
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/ScheduleManagementEnablersGrouping
ABB name: eira:ScheduleManagementEnablersGrouping
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/ScheduleManagementEnablersGrouping
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication

properties.value: Schedule Management Enablers ABB is a Grouping that enables the implementation of components and services to create, distribute and manage events at different scales inside a cloud platform.

The following implementation is an example on how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): Chronos is a replacement for cron. It is a distributed and fault-tolerant scheduler that runs on top of Apache Mesos that can be used for job orchestration. It supports custom Mesos executors as well as the default command executor. Thus by default, Chronos executes sh (on most systems bash) scripts. https://sourceforge.net/projects/chronos.mirror/

The Schedule Management Enablers Grouping ABB is salient for technical interoperability because it comprises the different services and tools that allow to organise and manage events within a platform for the delivery of a digital public service. It enables interoperability by allowing the management of events through a single point of access (for users and administrations) and the reuse of such information to an overall relationship between public administration and stakeholders.
